channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Redpill me on RV life — 36 replies — 144.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Redpill me on RV life

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:51 UTC

OP

Assume I wouldn't even have to drive very much, just around town randomly.

Early replies (first 3)

62003573
RV feels overkill, I think van life if you're single is better

62003620
trashy neighbors, lot fees are super gay, high maintenance, burn a lot of gas, easy to rob, difficult to sell, buying a used one is gross, and they depreciate quickly when you buy them new

62003633
>>62003573
Van seems like an overkill. I think sedan life if you're single is better.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003922
>>62003565
A RV like that costs as much as a small house, your reality will more look like what you call a trailer park

Youre maybe not allowed to park them anywhere you want or even in town

62004450
>>62003565
It only works if you are a traveling tradie that can secure work at a jobs site for months on end and stay at a camp ground. I see this with the data centers they are building in my area. Some of the electricians will have a 5th wheel trailer and use the truck to commute to and from the campground.

62004695
>>62003565
how is an rv good? just have a trailer rvs fucking suck

maybe even build it yourself since people try to scam you by marking them up too much

62004702
>>62004450
Make pallet camper and you can go anywhere

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QpO63kVjFb0

62004717
It's neither here nor there.
If you want something that big get a trailer or small house.
A van is a better idea, ideally a stealth one that isn't obvious you are living in it.
An RV or van that is clearly your home screams PLEASE COME HARASS AND/OR ROB ME.

62004741
Shit insulation if you live in the cold

62004743
>>62003565
The trick is to buy some cheap strip of land then get a used 30k camper trailer and put it on it. Boom, you've got a house with no monthly payments and all you have to do is pay taxes on the land every year.
The important part is to look very hard for a good deal. Someone I know got a very nice one, basically as large as a camper trailer can go, for 30k from a couple who didn't want it anymore. Never, ever buy from dealerships or buy new, they will vacuum money right out of your wallet.

62004847
>>62004695
You need one of them RVs that you can rig to the back of a pickup truck and not take up extra space beyond the truck's original space. There was a guy that lived in one of these in a parking lot for two years where I'm at.

62004973
>>62004847
that doesn’t seem like an improvement over living in a van

62005068
>>62003620
You act like this asshole isn't just going to park in front of random houses and industrial zone "no mans land".

62005158
>>62004450
this. some of the guys throwing up solar farms in western PA lived like this to save a portion of their per diem. might be worth it if you can secure a decent spot at the right price.

62005211
>>62003777
An apartment seems excessive to me; a tent near a sewer and a place where syringes can be found seems more suitable for a single person.

62006081
>>62003565
It can work in certain situations. Most of the time it's just better to rent with roommates or live with your parents. There are tons of youtubers living in vans, RVs, and even regular cars, some in cold climates.
>>62004741
You can always wear more layers for the cold. There are people who can sleep in tents in the Canadian woods during a snow. The real problem is heat. Assuming you can just spend time somewhere else during the day, if the night is above 30C good luck falling asleep. I once spent one summer without AC in a place where it's 43C during the day and around 28C-30C during the night. I was lucky enough to live on the ground floor so I was quite insulated during the day, and during the night I had to keep the main door open and only have a net aluminium door. I used to keep dozens of full water bottles so they could absorb heat during the day and radiate it out during the night using a fan. Shit like this is not feasible in a van. You will need a power source and use an AC.

62006094
>>62003565
You may as well just get a condo. At least it'll increase in value.

62006433
>>62006081

Yeah, people can technically not die in cold. You are 100% correct. It's not really a "gotcha!" moment if you wear 4 layers not to die when you exit your home when peopl in California literally never have to worry about their clothing. Still there is absolutely nothing you can do in -3 fahrenheit. You can walk from your house to a heated building. Without massive infrastructure people are just dead. You can enjoy a beverage outside at 95 fahrenheit. Heck, or just lie outside in the sun

t. finnish turan

62006466
>>62003565
The worst thing about living in an RV is where you have to shit. Unless you have a proper toilet you're going to hate whatever solution you get. You either shitting in a box you have to then empty by hand or you're shitting in a mini toilet that flushes into a tank under your feet that you have to empty or you're shitting into some weird variation with wood chips or blue Porto Potty water.

62006502
>>62004743
You have to be careful with local laws if you do this. A lot of places won't let you live like that. Helps if you can use a family member's place for your home address.

62008209
>>62003565
I don't think I could live my life taking a shit in a closet

62008225
>>62003565
>Zoomers trying to normalise living in vehicles again

62009772
>>62003565
living in your car is gross. I worked a security job 12 hour shift and had to use my car. Shit fuckin stank after awhile.

62009776
>>62008225
No we're just trying to avoid living in a pod

62009971
>>62005211
Fuck the tent. Live in the tunnel underneath the road.

62009981
>>62003565
you would be better off buying a small house in the middle of nowhere

62010078
>>62003565
If you get involved in a wreck with a vehicle, only your vehicle is ruined.

If you get involved in a wreck with an RV, your vehicle and your home are both ruined.

62010087
>>62003565
That thing is like living in a Barbie house with wheels.

62010125
If you own your own land, it's fine.

Private equity firms are buying up long-term stay RV lots to financially rape the already impoverished so it's not any cheaper than renting.

Full time travel is even worse. You'll need your own heavy duty truck and all the maintenance that entails. Fuel prices are spiking. Car insurance is spiking. Many RV lots aren't any cheaper than hotels. And if your RV is too large, you're not going to make it in and out of public land so boondocking is off the table.

62010148
>>62010078
>Sorry Stacey, I'd totally have you over tonight if my place wasn't in the shop.
lel

62010982
Get a pre covid built 26' max trailer. Nash, arctic fox, outdoor RV are some of the best ones. Now you can go boon docking anywhere on BLM and National Forest, there are so many places all over the country. Avoid staying in camp sites, see anon's post about it. I would only stay for a night if I'm travelling somewhere. Anything over 30', and you limit yourself severely regarding where you can park it. Water is available everywhere, even dumps, you can use ioverlander to find water, dumps, even boondocking spots. Get a Starlink for internet, you'll do great anon.

62010986
>>62003633
Why pay for automotive mobility at all? Nice coleman tent and a bike and you're mobile for a fraction of the price.

62011011
>>62003565
You aren’t allowed at Buc-ee’s which invalidates the entire point of OTR trucking jobs and RV life.

item.title: I literally put everything outside of my 401k to Gold a month ago. — 32 replies — 128.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
I literally put everything outside of my 401k to Gold a month ago.

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:51 UTC

OP

I literally put everything outside of my 401k to Gold a month ago. Keep with it or move to SGOV?
Holy fuck, thought I'd at least make a bit more playing it safe with it. Also put my entire IRA to Silver and Copper mining stocks (down like 30%)

Early replies (first 3)

62010094
>>62010018
>!SGOV
>SPXU
I am actually considering this

62010100
>>62010018
But you are holding an actual, real world commodity with utility that isn't internet funny money! You're the better, you're holding SOUND money!

62010109
>>62010018
If it makes you feel any better, all my friends at the Hindu temple did the same moves and are also down big. You're not alone, don't feel bad.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62010112
>>62010109
Praise vishnu pray to cow and gold pump big time

62010119
>i bought a giant pump of a commodity with little use besides as a store of value
>why am i losing money
really?

62010129
>>62010100
5000 kg needed for a scout rocket saaars!

62010132
>>62010119
You're not understanding, this is actual money and not at all like fiat it's supposed to go up because it's gold.

62010140
>>62010119
>>62010132
I thought it was the fastest horse. To me it seemed like we would only print more money every month, so it should keep the same trend

62010147
>>62010132
well irony aside it is safe and always goes up, when its at a fair price kek.
i dont get /biz/tards keep buying the top when actual gold stores refuse to buy at the recent market price

62010155
>>62010018
The pump for PMs and mining stocks ended. Now they will dump for the foreseeable future. If you aren't familiar with how these things work then stick to the "safe" investment advice that financial advisors give to the goyim.

62010156
>>62010018
there should be another PM bull market in the 2030's. just wait.

62010183
>>62010156
Diwali is late this year, November rather than October. So we'll get some relief there too

62010205
>>62010094
yeah, might just take a knee and do it too. SGOV for my brokerage and SPXU for my IRA. I hate losing money

62010228
>>62010018
Yeah man, sell low, buy high.

62010239
>>62010018
You might regret now, but you will be extremely happy in 15 years.

62010244
>>62010018
How fucking dumb can one person be. Kys OP.

62010326
>>62010018
Time horizon matters. If it's 10+ years, you'll be fine. If it's <3 years you could be in trouble. I think it will recover just fine in 3 years, but always plan for worse case scenarios.

But in general, there is a reason you shouldn't go all in on any one type of trade no matter how good it looks. Gold, silver and copper are all correlated so that was way to much risk in one sector. Lowering risk isn't just picking safe investments, it's taking into account that your thesis may be wrong.

62010500
>>62010018
bahahaha faggots on this board laughed at me for SGOV and chill, well who's laughing now?

62010515
>>62010326
Yeah, was planning to hold just until next year. Do you think I'm better off just selling to SGOV? Maybe SPXU within my IRA?
My whole thesis was that stocks down, commodities will go up, especially with money printing coming from this war, but seems the big players are cashing out to something else
>>62010500
I'm still mostly SGOV. My entire IRA is gold/silver/copper mining stocks though. I might just sell off my brokerage back to SGOV though and dca to BTC when its below 50k

62010523
>>62010515
Here's the IRA. Tempted to sell off all to SPXU

62010546
>>62010515
>>62010523
And here's the 401k. Open to any suggestions. I bend the knee guys. I'm not able to make money off investments whatsoever. I'm just good at my job, that's it.

62010689
>>62010018
Retard

62010751
>>62010515
>>62010523
>>62010546
You simply should not be investing in miners if you can't handle a 30% drawdown. That's just normal movement for them. You should also not touch spxu. Shorting the market is difficult even for skilled traders. It's also leveraged so large drawdowns are normal even in moves going your direction and you've proven you can't handle that. You seem took risk adverse, so what you should do is decide on a well diversified portfolio based on allocation percentages to a few simple funds and rebalance on a set schedule and just forget about it. Fidelity has baskets that you can use to automatically do this for you, it costs like $5 a month.

62010788
>>62010100
True gold bugs buy the real thing. You’re supposed to liquid enough to hold the real thing forever. You can get a ton but there’s such a thing as “enough” for what will happen down the road. Going all in is retarded speculation. Didn’t they teach you this stuff?

62010799
>>62010788
Meant for
>>62010018

62010810
>>62010751
Thanks for the reply. Do you think I should keep the miners then?
Also, what do you think of just going all in SGOV and buying back BTC later this year? That's the only investment I've ever made good money from by buying in bear markets and holding a few years til bull markets

62010842
>>62010018
>>62010100
>>62010788
you should diversify between stocks (which are good when things are stable but worthless if SHTF) and gold/silver (which won't gain as much as stocks when the economy is stable but will have value if SHTF)
and also guns/ammo.

62010843
So you basically got in at the very top after the normie mania drove the price radically higher, not exactly a good timing if you're looking for a quick buck.
However you'll be fine long term, but it might be 3-5 years for us to reach the top again.

62010855
>>62010843
3-5 years just to break even? I rather just get out to treasury bills at that point, then buy BTC

62010859
Yeah sell so this correction can end please thank you.

62011004
Have you seen the 10yr treasury bond yield? Only a matter of time before the fed reluctantly has to cut rates, run the money printers, and buy up debt!

62011016
>>62011004
So, then keep everything as is?

item.title: Finally feel financially comfortable to date… All my dates so far have been asking for expensive steak houses like Ruth… — 17 replies — 68.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Finally feel financially comfortable to date… All my dates so far have been asking for expensive steak houses like Ruth…

Open thread • Replies: 17 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:52 UTC

OP

Finally feel financially comfortable to date…

All my dates so far have been asking for expensive steak houses like Ruth Chris/Capital Grill or sushi…

What the fuck how do normie men afford this shit? I’m gonna have to sell some stocks/crypto to pay off this month CC bill. I’ve only gotten 1 shitty blow job and tit groping from this shit.

Early replies (first 3)

62002801
Thanks for playing

62002854
>>62002594

simpin' aint easy.

62003014
>>62002594
Find a Japanese girl who loves the cheapest noodles

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003026
>>62002594
Its the price you pay for being a coomtard simp. Youre not made to make it, sorry.

62003250
>>62002594
>Letting the bitch looking for a free meal pick the resturant
You're the man, you tell them where you guys are eating, not the other way around. Also
>Being a traditional man by paying for everything while the women refuse to be traditional women
Never gonna make it anon, equality means they pay half the time

62003410
>>62002594
LOL

My chase sapphire has an 8500 CC who do you think you are posting this cricket dick 5k BALANCE HAHAHA

62003534
>>62002594
Protip: you're a low sexual market value male and going to a third world country for blowjobs and pussy would strengthen your position

62004101
>>62002594
You are dating the wrong girls for the wrong reason. You should be grateful to learn this about them before wasting time and money and heartache.

62004187
>>62002594
What the fuck, I thought that was a checking account balance. Using credit cards in 2026 is big goy. Also you’re spending on other pointless shit, there’s no way you racked up 5k in 20 days going on dates with bimbos at corporate chain restaurants

62006841
>>62002594
sushi is the secret cheap option

62006849
>>62003410
I got a Citi Diamond card with a 16,800 limit. Crazy to think about since my first card from around 2017 was a Capital One card that is still stuck at 500 and they refuse to increase it.

62007466
>>62002594
Just get a membership to a local attraction that lets you bring a +1 and bring all your dates there.

Theres a botanical garden a mile from my house. Tickets are normally $30 a person but a yearly pass for 2 is $110. I walk there for dates and then get the woman to drive me home which usually results in her coming in for a marginal cost of $0

62007536
>>62002594
Don't be a simp

62010502
>>62002594
My lust died the second I wrote the average feminine nature on paper and stratified it according to its moral nature. I am now convinced that the only thing you are supposed to do as a soul, as a man is to wrestle your own flesh into submission, find God and develop a prosperous and altruistic relationship with our creator in order to escape this prison. 99% of women are half humans with no souls, everything they strive for and enjoy is ultimately evil in its nature. The very core of femininity is absolute evil that seeks to devour good men with the same glee and joy you'd get from stomping out some dorky kids sand castle.

Once I realized why women are the way they are, it's like I've finally been enlightened as to why hermit monks become hermits in the first place. It's all a lie, its all a joke. This planet is a prison, women are archons who genuinely don't give a fuck about anything but they have to pretend to have interests beyond themselves just so men don't become suspicious and notice the huge disparity in the nature of each gender. It's not a complimentary nature, it's completely one-sided and parasitic. The entire role of women is to entrap a mans life force and pull another soul onto this false reality incarnation trap. That's all they were designed to do. Satan takes a hold of their hivemind vessels with comedic ease and no resistance. They are all beyond corrupt, they fall apart once a month and endure pain unless they fulfill their ill-natured task. There is nothing in this world worth striving for besides Gods love. There is absolutely NOTHING in this world that is worth your place in heaven.

62010703
>>62010502
>. This planet is a prison, women are archon
you've been on /x too much

62010940
>>62002594
Anon for 5k, you could've flown to and from japan and gotten multiple escorts and a better tiem

62011022
Good. People like you who give them a chance deserve to be fleeced. Thanks for paying sarr!

item.title: Why don't more retail traders try to learn the techniques of quantitative finance? — 8 replies — 32.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why don't more retail traders try to learn the techniques of quantitative finance?

Open thread • Replies: 8 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:48 UTC

OP

Why don't more retail traders try to learn the techniques of quantitative finance? I'm trying to do it myself, and outside of the difficulty of getting practical experience and actually applying the theory, the theory itself is not that hard. I think it's because retail traders overall are just really dumb people, but there are still many who have the capacity to make it work.

The math is accessible to anyone with an engineering, math, or physics degree. Basically it's mostly linear algebra and statistics. Those companies are hiring people with just bachelors degrees in CS for quant positions will tell you that. Yes they are hiring the best new grads with 4.0 GPAs from MIT but they still don't somehow just know PhD level math. Stochastic calculus is a meme from 20 years ago when all quants did was work on the sell side pricing exotic derivatives for banks using models with stochastic differential equations. You don't need it and it's not even really helpful, and there are rigorous books on that deal with all the mathematical prerequisites anyways if you really wanted to learn it.

It's not impossible and you don't need to be a super genius to use quant strategies to benefit your retail trading. You just need to be a decent programmer and have decent applied math knowledge. The entire mystique around quant is just gatekeeping trying to keep you from making money so that only hedge funds can use these strategies and models, because they don't want retail investors to make it. They don't want you to be armed with the tools they have so you can keep being exit liquidity.

Early replies (first 3)

62010454
>it's not fucking rocket science, but none of you would know about rocket science because you're all fucking retards probably

62010482
Thanks Anon

62010520
>Why isn't anyone doing what PHD level mathematicians backed by billions of dollars of capital are doing? The math is right in front of you!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62010545
I'm gonna calculate the hecking bivariate gaussian distribution of the implied vomma aaargghghhhhfg fuck I just shitted myself

62010567
>>62010187
the market isn't normally distributed. that doesn't mean to use laplace, that means to look for the finger under the cloth stepping outside of brownian motion. thanks for reading my ted talk.

62010960
>>62010187
I find gemini 3.1 pro is good enough to check the math if you frame the problem correctly, and it's fairly easy to learn the theory from books.
The problem it's like competing with poos for twitter impressions. It's a penny for million business. The big firms are doing it on a large scale and no commisions, and can scrap some profit. A single investor will hardly break even after commisions.

You can have some luck selling options, if you can price them correctly to sell insurance for a small premium.
But that fucks with your risk profile, so might as well go 2x SPY and hope for the best.

There is very little free money on the market, you have to pick bets and take risk if you want to outperform an ETF.

62010972
>>62010960
>perform like ETF
quiet boomer

62010988
>>62010187
because a true artist can click buy and sell based on feelings and outperform any automated math goy autist hft thing
it's true but you will convince yourself u need to learn high level calculus or something
youve already convinced yourself of this, havent you?

I GOT MARGIN CALLED

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:51 UTC

OP

I GOT MARGIN CALLED

Early replies (first 3)

62010889

ouch.. goodluck anon

62010895
>>62010866
just delete the app, bro, all the data gets deleted along with it

62010898
>>62010866
this is good. use it as motivation to come back stronger. losing money is a valuable learning experience.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62011006
>>62010866
Just don't answer the call, let them go to voicemail

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 86 replies — 47.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 86 • Updated: 2026-03-20 18:07 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

61984347
>>61981809
It went down

61984420
>>61984347

>>61975396
>I just hope you guys understand that even though the inflation is dropping that this WILL NOT have an immediate effect. Stuff takes time. If you believe that the price will sky rocket after the inflation drop then you're in for a disappointing experience.
The APY is still 12% desu. It'll take another 10h 40min until the new era rolls around (https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview).

61984446
dot sucks

61984449
>>61984446
>dot sucks
That's great. Women suck too, that's why we love them. DOT is really good at sucking, it'll give you the best BJ

61986812
Tomorrow the inflation will actually drop

61990080
>>61986812

61992305
>>61990080
It's still going up

61992626
>>61992305
ETF flows. New buyers. Selling is slowing. Holy shit. Become a DOThead

61993232
>>61992626
The ETF inflows are kind of mid though https://www.21shares.com/en-us/products-us/tdot. Only 11.5m$, doesn't really affect the mcap too much really, but oh well. It's a start. More ETFs will be coming out

61995431
The golden bull run might have just started. How are we feeling?

61995436
>>61995431
good when return @ 6$

61996036
>>61993232
Can't expect a massive inflow like BTC. Had a decent pump already.

The technicals look stable. Advice. Jump in now while you can

61997405
100$ end of bull run

61997626
Just bought in, life savings. When does it hit $10?

61998660
>>61932834

This shit is going to 100 dollars isnt it

61998672
>>61997626
Oh shit anon. We should've told you that if YOU buy then it's going to 0$. You should sell and then after you sell we'll GIGA ULTRA MEGA PUMP to 100$+

61998866
>>61998660
Yes. Yes it is

61999089
>>61998866
Did you buy?

62000247
>>61999089
Of course. 100 DOT holder reporting in

62000433
>>62000247
It's over guys. This guy doomed us. Because he bought it's going to 0$. Please sell before I put my DOT into my cars brakes.

62004150
>>62000247
The moment you bough the whole market started dumping.

62007000
100$ end of bull run. Is it possible to keep the thread alive for 1 full month? 15 days to go!

62007009
How much are they paying you jeets to post here?

62007046
>>62007009
Well... The question is... How much do you want to get paid to jeet like we do? We can easily mog your jobs salary. JEETMAXXING

62007254
>>62007009
Zero. Plus im white

62008295
>>62007254
Good one dear fren Rajakesh Pujakistan. That'll convince him. Now post your white hand with a timestamp. Picrel is me and my white hand sar

62010139
So... When's JAM coming out guys? Those fuckers stopped doing the monthly development calls

62010191
>>62010139
If you're not a bot, here's a question: why constantly humiliate yourself in public like this? It's embarrassing. Nobody cares about the pedochain and Gayvin.

62010262
>>62010191
I'm really glad you brought that up anon. Great question! And if I want to answer it properly, like you deserve, my good sar, then I'll need a bit more information
>why constantly humiliate yourself in public like this? It's embarrassing. Nobody cares about the pedochain and Gayvin.
What do you mean by constantly humiliate myself? Can you help me understand that

item.title: Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake — 52 replies — 33.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Still Selling BTC? Big Mistake

Open thread • Replies: 52 • Updated: 2026-03-20 18:21 UTC

OP

I’ve been telling folks here not to sell BTC and now they’re buying at the top.

>When are you gonna learn your lesson, folks? HODL

Early replies (first 3)

61996853
>>61996755
rich kid but black

61996883
>>61996853
What's the problem?

61998635
>>61996755
Sell and wait for 10k per btc

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62003111
>>61996755
If you’re in for the long term, short-term dips aren’t worth worrying about. Just stay in control and HODL.

62003117
BTC might retrace a bit, but don’t panic sell. Plan your moves, don’t let fear drive trades.

62003136
>>62003088
It's 0.07% APR. And you will be sending your BTC to an anonymous system whose owner pinky promises to send you your money back if you ask nicely.

62003422
>>62003088
It’s possible, just look into it yourself. you’ll get it. Self custody is the advantage.

62003430
>>61996755
Let the goyim deal in the crypto world.

62003444
>>62003136
“Anonymous system” lol. It’s literally built around cryptographic guarantees, not trust. APR is low, sure, but better than nothing. This isn’t sending BTC to some random yield farm.

62003448
>>61998652
He's a great kiddo

62003660
>>62003430
Bitcoin keeps improving. Lightning, Taproot, and other upgrades make BTC faster, cheaper, and more usable, confidence grows, price follows.

62003668
Market sentiment drives crypto hard. A little media buzz, some whales buying, and suddenly BTC is pumping. FOMO is real.

62003679
>>62003444
The only defi right now is ethereum

62003695
>>62003117
Whats your price target?

62003910
>>62003679

62003914
>>62003111

62004044
>>61996755

62004059

62004175

62004400

62005462
>>62003660

62006331
>>62003668

62003963

62004017

62004272

62004291
>>62004017
Dude people are so fucking dumb. "Duhhhhh gold is a safe haven so that means it has to go up when I want it to".

Dipshit - think for a second. Ppl spent years buying gold in case it "all goes to shit" and now it's starting to "all go to shit" and you're shocked they're selling their end of the world hedge? How fucking dumb are you outside of here be fr.

Look at what gold did from 2006-2012. Jesus christ some ppl never fucking even try to learn.

62004296
>>62004017
The end of the modern world was priced in. Thanks for playing. Its just a rock, bro.

62004302
>>62004017
Sry but what really pissed me off was you talking ab deleveraging even though margin requirements have been going down this was really ur fault if u think about it

62004309
>>61999090

62004600
>>62004017
>I mean the world is on the brink of collapse why would you dump gold now

62005322
Idk how to dollar cost average so whatever I put in I just buy by the percentage it went down

62005367
>>62004600
>paper

62008264
>>61998888
gold is unruggable, small fluctuations like this dont matter

62009069
>>61998888
nice quads retard. also this points to the big players trying to close the exits by putting up a smoke screen. the system is imploding and they don't want anyone running for actual hard assets or assets outside of the system. this is a warning from them showing their hand. they are scared as fuck.

the rips we saw in January were breaches in a the dam. they are trying to shore that up with tape. it's still leaking.

62009076
>>62008264
>gold is unruggable
this in absolute terms. but in the short terms it easily shakes off paper handed retards who babysit the ticker and shit themselves when the price drops 3% and immediately sell like the losers they are.

62009209
just buy the dips

62009994
I literally put everything outside of my 401k to this a month ago. Keep with it or move to SGOV? Holy fuck, thought I'd at least make a bit more playing it safe with it

62010048
>>62008264

62010070
>>61998888

62010074
>>61998888

item.title: They flipped the switch — 36 replies — 17.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
They flipped the switch

Open thread • Replies: 36 • Updated: 2026-03-20 17:53 UTC

OP

Many of the intelligent anons here already suspected March was a pivot point. Well, here it is. The silent flip. Chainlink has permeated the gates of Washington and has considerable influence at this point that was built carefully and mostly out of the spotlight, because regulatory ambiguity around digital assets made overt positioning impossible.

Understand that in groups where powerful men meet and make critical decisions, Chainlink is being positioned as connective infrastructure for the next generation of finance, state-level coordination, and machine-verifiable systems. Monetary policy rails, yes we all know that. But also cyber defense architecture, government identity-adjacent workflows, and potentially even admin systems where the ability to be audited lives in the public forum.

The recent announcement from the SEC/CFTC that deems Chainlink as a commodity is the green light. I can tell you that Economics 2.0 will be announced shortly and the slides are literally being prepared as we speak. The next major milestone after that is staking expansion this summer.

There is still a question about how much friction individual states can still create on the margins (CA, NJ, SC, WI) but the decision makers don't care about that. Hope you don't live in any such places. Most people will not understand what they are looking at until after the repricing. That is how these transitions always work. By the time the headlines catch up, the asymmetry is gone.

Early replies (first 3)

62007047
WW3 is starting and you're still shilling about this gay, irrelevant scamcoin.

62007066
>>62007042
You’re going to have to get a job chief. Typing out your delusions on an indoor kite flying forum isn’t going to pay the bills. Remember, eye contact and firm handshake

62007080
Where's insider anon's larp again?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007325
>>62007042
Shitcoin scam

62007348
>the slides are literally being prepared as we speak

Kek

62007387
>>62007042
>economics 2.0
wouldn't that be 3.0 now? lol

how long till this shit actually goes live - i.e. either one of dtcc, swift, txse starts transacting with ccip? within a year? 2 years? 2030?

>staking expansion
can you tell sergey to throw us long-term stakers a bone? the latest round of build rewards has been complete dogshit, especially with the timegated unlock (FOLKS rugged right after cube allocation ended). how about some SVR fees and weighed more favorably by time staked? help some of the poorfags(me) lube up a little for believing in this project through this market

62007580
>>62007387
adding to the last point on "loyalty rewards". this is probably the best time to do it:
>before normies flood in and dilute the market share of link resulting in weaker buy power for us(me)
>SVR fees are captured in ETH, which makes for good conversion to link before dominance reverses bigly
>last BUILD reward season sliced too generously for late stakers. stop withholding rewards for so long such that the next gen of latefags are gonna cannibalize us
>an actual way to thank linkies implicitly through whatever mathematical formulas that you wouldnt spell out anyway
>self-filtering test by linkies who would sell immediately

62007657
>>62007042
>42
>jew id
>link schizo posting

62007720
AHA! THE FAKER RETURNS!
BEGONE, CRETIN!
TIS I! THE TRUE ARTEMIS!

62007736
>>62007042
You forgot to end with
>Heh, I'm so excited for you crazy kids!

62007869
>>62007042
Artemis-sama, how drastic will the price increase be this summer? What would be the minimum amount of link to never wage slave again and live a simple life? To make a positive impact in this world even in the slightest?

62007874
>THIS SUMMER COPE
IT WAS ALWAYS
>TONIGHT!
FOOL!

>t.Artemis

62007879
>>62007047
this world is so gay that i could see some random shitcoin like link mooning during WW3

62007881
I'm so glad this is still a chainlink board with daily posts about my favorite fat billionaire Russians business. Just kidding no I'm not please kys.

62007902
3.3 mill inflow on ETFS today.

62007905
>>62007902
the most of ANY crypto ETF today btw

62007938
so when do i sell and at what price

62007973
>>62007905
holy samefag

62008011
>>62007902
ur mom had 3.3 mill inflow today. of semen.
#rekt

62008018
>>62007387
>long-term stakers a bone?
eat shit buddy. Many of us can't even get in the pool. Be happy you've gotten the 4.5% and some random memecoins.

62008356
>>62008018
congratulations on being a latefag. you'll have to make do with sdl

62008363
>>62007042
Finally

My ducks are in a row

62008438
The copium is real in this thread. It's inevitable that we will transition to digital currency and AI oversight. You know it. I know it. It's just a matter of when. And I believe OP's prediction is a rational prediction.

62008468
>$2 above where it was when I first started researching crypto
I figured back then it'd be something I rotated gains into as it seemed to have long-term potential and it seems that's panning out better than I thought.

62008498
>>62007042

Holy shit I think this may be a sign

62008529

62008569
>>62007042

you have no panache, no flair for drama
you have no personality

62008595
what the make it by next year stack?

62010029
>>62008569

62010118

Ya'll deserve to lose money if you dont understand recent history

item.title: I have noticed people tend to 1. be talented with their hands and make money working a trade 2. are effortlessly intelli… — 21 replies — 10.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
I have noticed people tend to 1. be talented with their hands and make money working a trade 2. are effortlessly intelli…

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-03-20 17:55 UTC

OP

I have noticed people tend to

1. be talented with their hands and make money working a trade

2. are effortlessly intelligent and get a higher education and are able to secure a well pay career

3. are neither intelligent or good with their hands, but have extremely good social skills, and can socialise their way into obtaining resources

4. have some kind of unique talent or physical features the market values and are able to monitize it

5. are born into well off or rich families, who either have their living situation permanently funded so have no need to actually compete out in the real world and just coast by with the families wealth


But what if you have none of the above? Because thats me right now, and im in my 30's.

I worked a really really min wage poverty job, but I spent all my savings from working trying to "invest" (ie: gamble) in high risk plays like crypto.

Do I just KMS if things get really bad like becoming homeless? Doesn't seem to be anyway out of this very low status chud peasant lifestyle

Early replies (first 3)

62007043
I think you belong in

6. begin a complex criminal syndicate dealing with black markets as a source of income (i.e narcotics manufacturing, arms smuggling or human trafficking)

62007049
>>62007002
Can i be 3 if im short and ugly? I always talked non stop as a kid and got in trouble at school constantly for never shutting up

62007059
>>62007043
that sounds fun, honestly prison seems so bad i rather just die than go to prison for 20+ years..

if the only threat of 6 was dying, i would legit be doing it. but prison for life is a fate worse than death so no thanks

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62007063
>>62007043
Yeah my bad I forgot to mention the criminal path.

I am not cold and ruthless enough to deal with the people in that lifestyle though

62007071
>>62007059
fine. suit yourself, you said it yourself. You have a dead-end job with no credentials, you cope with gambling. if you wont take risks then you'll do nothing. nothing happens if nothing happens.

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Paul_Le_Roux

62007072
>>62007049
One of the popular kids at my school was super fat. Charisma 100

62007371
>>62007071
im not op nigga wtf u talking about

62007380
>>62007072
mirin

62007939
Just be law account medic teacher engineer

62007989
>>62007043
Criminal path is having no life at all. You always have to watch your back. You are just surviving.

62008007
>>62007002
Idk man.

You should probably take a few professional IQ and Big 5 Personality tests. It might help you understand who you are as a person.

I consider myself quite knowledge about a diverse number of topics, but I don't view myself as very intelligent in an abstract mathematical problem solving sense.

I'm 32 and I've worked a basic factory labor job in the rural Midwest for 14 years. I'm also in the top 7% of wealth for people my age. I don't have a degree and I didn't get lucky on crypto or meme stocks. I've just been very persistent with saving, investment and frugality. My brain is just wired that way, because my parents and peers didn't push any this stuf towards me. I figured it out on my own.

IQ = 122

OCEAN:
O = 96th percentile
C = 98th percentile
E = 54th percentile
A = 2nd percentile
N = 60th percentile

If you met me in real life, you'd first view me as stoic and respectful. If you got to know me more, you'd probably think I'm an asshole because I'm blunt, callous, show contempt towards people I think are morally bankrupt or lazy, and only show empathy to people who I think deserve it and the empathy is result based, not emotionally based. You would also find me very trustworthy and reliable, because I won't lie to you, backstab you and I stick to my word.

Do/Think whatever you want with that information.

62008043
>>62007002
Fag.
>>62008007
Based autist.

62008165
>>62007002
According to the propaganda you just need to work harder and you'll be rich in no time. Sorry man I wish it was like that. Keep your savings and don't gamble. Try to live like a monk. Fight for your basic needs but don't gamble.

62008182
>>62007989
Thats what your mummy taught you but what do you really think?

62008919
>>62007002
Git gud

62008934
>>62007002
Youre virtueless.

62008940
>>62007002
Do you parents not even let you live with them? How are you in danger of homelessness?

62010172
>>62008182
That's what I think. Blue collar crimes are not worth it. You gotta have low iq to do any of it. When you travel around the world, you don't want any dirt on your record.

62010184
>>62007989
After a certain point you stop being worried about arrest and start worrying about assassination

62010197
>>62010172
On the contrary you got to be super intelligent to do crime. Cant even steal a cellphone without some exploit to get past the pin. I mean theres selling drugs but that only gets more savage as time goes on. Used to hot wire a car and drive off now you need le rolling codes. Its like you need a pocket hacker for everything.

62010201
>>62010184
assassination is better than 30+ years in prison

item.title: >world go bad gold must always go up >WTF WHY GOLD NO GO UP?!??! — 31 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
>world go bad gold must always go up >WTF WHY GOLD NO GO UP?!??!

Open thread • Replies: 31 • Updated: 2026-03-20 16:36 UTC

OP

>world go bad gold must always go up
>WTF WHY GOLD NO GO UP?!??!
Is the most brain dead retarded take imaginable. These retards fail to understand the price of gold is also linked to it's industrial use.

Early replies (first 3)

62006969
>>62006961
These are the same type of people who bought gamestop btw.

62006976
>>62006969
digits... checked

62006979
>>62006961
Probably a horde of cunts started leveraging themselves long up the ass when the war started.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62006990
what do we do bros?

62006994
>>62006990
just cash out, what you want gold to go to $20k? are you fucking delusional? fucking psychotic people on here.

62007032
>>62006961
gold is a speculative asset.

62007108
>>62006990

or
>baghold for 20? years

62007658
>>62006961
>it's industrial use.
learn grammar

62007712
>OOOYYYYYYYYY GOOD CRASHED TO THE PRICE IT WAS ONLY 1 MONTH AGO SELL SELL SELL SELL

62007753
>>62006961
Governments are selling gold to buy Iranian and Russian oil

62007795
>>62006961
Might be a good time to buy if you have some extra liquidity.
If the economy does go down the shitter like everyone says, its going to be more money printing.

62007829
>>62006976
not digits you fucking faggot

62007832
>>62006961
I voted for this

62007890
>>62006961
People need cash to buy up other discounted assets.

62007922
>>62007753
oil seems to be a foolproof investment

62007928
>>62006961
China dumping their stack to reap profits and pay for the war theyre about to join

62007949
>>62006961
Good and silver have crashed hard during an economic downturn. This is temporary. They spike back up after the money printing to keep industries running.

62007959
>>62006994
And whats in your portfolio

62009136
>>62007922
> world government tightens emissions regulations tanking the price

now what chief?

62009162
once gold goes below 2500 i may slurp

62009254
>>62006961
>but but the industrial use of gold!
makes up 10% of the usage at most

62009264
>>62006961
Markets predicted geo political uncertainty before it happened and it shot up too much. Everyone tried to do it before actual uncertainty and then bow it happened, it's falling.

62009299
>>62007949
how long does it usually take?

62009322
>>62009299
Longer than you can wait.

62009359
the markets are a casino
all you're doing is gambling
the house always wins

62009589
>>62006961
>the price of gold is also linked to it's industrial use.
Troll thread

62009613
>gold crashes to the price I last bought at
looks like it's time to buy more

62009655
Economy is fake, take this opportunity to buy more gold because the ones that want to have all the gold wants you to sell it.

62009662
>>62009359
Pretty much, you should know by now who's the house.

62009810
>>62006961
>gold has kept a high value for thousands of years
>goes down a couple of days
>HAH APPARENTLY GOLD IS WORTH EXACTLY ZERO
just go outside for a bit dude

62009821
>>62009589
yeah, before the 1700s, gold was priced at zero because it wasn't use industrially, we all know that!

item.title: career advice?? — 4 replies — 9.3/hr
item.content:encoded:
career advice??

Open thread • Replies: 4 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:30 UTC

OP

I graduated high school in 2019. I completed a 3 year BA in linguistics in 4 years in 2023. Then I fucked off to China to teach English for 2 years for peanuts. Don't know what else to do so I'm going to do a teaching masters for 2 years so I'm qualified to teach primary school. Primary school teachers make 63k to 91k USD once experienced in my country so I think the salary is not too bad. Anything else I can do with my life? I don't particularly want to do it but it pays well and it's easy to get a job. Honestly I'd rather just do a job where I don't have to talk to people.

Early replies (first 3)

62010739
>linguistics
>in the age of AI
Kek

62010765
>>62010143
Would

62010824
>tried to teach english in japan in college years ago
>they wouldn't take me until I finished my degree and wouldn't pay for flight/accommodations so I needed money
>saved money, studied japanese and tried again in 2020 but we know what happened
>now I see ads with jeets on them advertising "no degree required" jap-eng teachers
Why does the universe hate me
Anyway linguistics is useless except for teaching so become a teacher, I don't make the rules.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62010841
>>62010143
>I don't particularly want to do it but it pays well and it's easy to get a job.
>Honestly I'd rather just do a job where I don't have to talk to people.
You don't have these options, you're already 25. If you ever let yourself think "oh man, I wanna think about it for a while" you'll wake up. Boom. Another 7 years will have gone by and you'll be 32. Kids will call you 'unc' and 'cheugy' How did that happen you'll ask. I should have gotten the job, saved up and invested all my money into Bitcoin when it was under 100k you'll say, now that bitcoin is over 250k.

Society advertises this idea that your 20s are for 'findin yourself', if you slack off even a ltitle bit you'll be over 30 and asking yourself how you can find your way back to 20 to fix all your mistakes.

item.title: CORN - the next golden trade. — 7 replies — 8.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
CORN - the next golden trade.

Open thread • Replies: 7 • Updated: 2026-03-20 19:08 UTC

OP

Hormuz is shut and half the world’s urea and a third of ammonia exports are stuck behind it. Ammonia went from $850 to $1,050 a ton in three weeks, Qatar’s fertilizer plants are offline, India and Bangladesh are shutting theirs down too because they can’t get gas feedstock anymore. There’s no strategic fertilizer reserve like there is for oil, nobody planned for this. Corn needs more nitrogen than any other major crop and at these input costs it literally doesn’t make money at $4.50 a bushel, so farmers are going to rotate acres into basedbeans which fix their own nitrogen. Less corn planted means less corn harvested in September means price goes up. USDA Prospective Plantings report March 31 is the catalyst. Even if the war ends tomorrow the fertilizer still hasn’t shipped, it takes 30 days on the water plus weeks to reach inland farms, and planting season is NOW. The supply destruction is already locked in regardless of what happens in the Middle East. Pure agriculture math at this point. CORN, CXRN if you want 2x. (but it’s a small etf, just a few million dollars large) As of time of posting, you have an hour left before markets close for the weekends. Also, stop trading American oil markets and for fucks sake at least trade BNO if you insist on remaining an NPC. Good luck retards; you’re welcome.

Early replies (first 3)

62010641
I knew my piss jugs were worth saving up

62010643
Youre welcome for what? Now i got to buy corn futures to afford corn ff

62010661
> If that were true anon, wouldn’t funds be buying it?

Look at the price chart with one day candles fuckface. They are. It just hasn’t broken out yet. But it will.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62010666
What's the world going to be like when retail investors rush in to exploit commodity markets, enriching themselves in equities while simultaneously impoverishing themselves and their fellow citizens in the goods and services they buy?

62010677
>>62010643
Hey at least you’ll be eating. Whatever wealth is redefined as in post apocalyptic 2027, i want to be part of it.

62010688
>>62010666
>based digits of prophecy

I agree that the next retard retail lemming rush is into commodities of all types, and it will be awful.

62010702
>>62010688
Holy fucking trip dubs. I can’t remember the last time I saw that shit.

item.title: Anyone else actually made it here? — 16 replies — 7.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else actually made it here?

Open thread • Replies: 16 • Updated: 2026-03-20 17:52 UTC

OP

>finally "make it"
>multimillionaire at 31, doctor, taking home $700k post tax in UK, probably top 0.1% if not higher
>it's literally just shit

Anyone else know this feel? Dating is impossible if you want a serious partner because effectively no one is close. You don't need to do anything which is ok but it's so easy to become completely unproductive. Buying stuff kind of pointless even on average UK salary can get a comfy lifestyle. No challenge or anything just pure freedom which is really disconcerting.

Before anyone says it, no I was not born rich. Grew up with abusive single mother on welfare and father thinks she messed about with birth control on purpose to get the bennies, fairly sure this is true. Closest family member is my aunt who is mentally ill. It's a very start contrast going from total no hope loser to current life in 15 years.

Early replies (first 3)

62009296
>>62009286
>$700k post tax in UK
No they aren't lmao

62009318
>>62009296
This obviously is not from medicine I don't practise any more. Income from trading and legally have tax set up so I pay basically nothing.

62009405
stupid lying fucking nigger
at 31 you're barely a specialized doctor and not earning well yet and this fucking faggot says
>I don't practise any more
when he would literally be too young to even seriously practice. Dumbest larp today

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62009438
>>62009286
The Great Regret

62009452
There are not a lot of books and/or content to find written to address the problems of people in the top slice, however some stuff is there if you invest time finding it.
Psychology and political science and maybe other fields, if done well, are all about acknowledging all levels of human competence and idiocy in their model. Find a chicky smart like that.

62009461
>>62009405
So much dumb shit...

In UK you typically become fully qualified doctor at 23/24. This is the age you start practising as a doctor, graduating at 31 is clearly later than typical

After that the most common path is GP (FM in USA) and you can easily be done by 28. 31 is young but possible for a consultant in another specialty.

62009522
>>62009286
>working
>"made it"
no you didnt

62009548
>>62009286
shit larp desu

62009573
>>62009286
>taking home $700k post tax in UK
maybe stop larping?
muh huh I make a million a year and I'm so depressed!

62009658
>>62009286
I made it, had 30M, succumbed to 'just one more 2x' syndrome and now have just 7M after taxes, or maybe a bit less after today's nigger selloff. I'm still levered to the tits trying hit that one last 2x, even after going down 75%. I fully expect to run myself down to my last 2M which is locked up in index funds that are almost a decade old and I told myself I'd never gamble with under any circumstances. I should probably set up a custodian or wealth manager now to protect those funds from myself because I know left to my own devices that when I've burnt through the very last of my gambling stack I'll probably cannibalize that nest egg regardless what I promised myself.

Life was kind of nice for 9 months before 10/10 though. NGL.

62009709
>>62009286
You failed at "Do no harm" so it's karmic justice

62009739
you're lying. physicians make like 200-300k max in NHS.

62009815
>>62009286
>Still in the UK
There's your problem. It's a shitty, miserable country whether you're poor or rich.

62009851
>>62009658
> 7M after taxes
> after taxes
In all seriousness, this is how I know you aren't larping. It's funny to see people say they have 2mil, when in reality it's 1.6 at best.

Imo, you should just take 6 out and then play with the 1 left.

8 mil is still $320,000 a year - what are going to do with more than that that's worth risking going back to a job for?

Flex wars will never be won, there is literally always a bigger fish, so the real question is actual spending.

62010120
>>62009286
Why not make it your life’s passion to advocate for and help poor people?
Like the difference in wages

62010189
>>62009658
That's enough to never have to work for the rest of your life
